behavior_summary: Implements the guard NPC's short inspection reaction (GUARD1::look), proximity enter/exit hooks (GUARD1::enterFastArea / GUARD1::leaveFastArea), and the full conversation/talk handler (GUARD1::use).
GUARD1::look: when a player inspects the guard, the NPC barks a short identity line; if the guard is dead it barks a 'dead guardsman' message instead.

GUARD1::enterFastArea / leaveFastArea: manage per-actor fast-area registration/cleanup via an engine helper (FREE::2A56) and spawn a brief helper (U8P_GUARD1::1033) when a living actor enters proximity.

GUARD1::use: presents a branching dialog tree (via Item::ask) driven by persistent global flags (guard1met, guard1pissed, guard1town, guard1job, toranDead), player responses and random variation (urandom). It controls speech (Item::bark), may set the Avatar into stasis, schedules animations/movements (via spawn and Npc::pathfind), and updates guard state flags.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD1::look immediately checks Npc::isDead and selects one of two short barks before returning.
U8P_GUARD1::enterFastArea checks Npc::isDead; if alive it calls FREE::2A56 (registration) then spawn U8P_GUARD1::1033; dead guards bypass registration/spawn.
U8P_GUARD1::leaveFastArea calls FREE::2A56 to deregister/cleanup and returns.

U8P_GUARD1::use builds a dynamic menu (slist) for Item::ask; selected choices are string-compared to known options (e.g., 'Bye ', 'What is your name?', 'I am the Avatar!', 'Open the gate!') and drive branching: replies produce barks, may set global flags, may set Avatar stasis, and spawn timed/animation helper processes.

Dialog composition in U8P_GUARD1::use depends on toranDead (adds/removes 'Open the gate!' option) and guard1met/guard1pissed to choose first-time vs subsequent/angry responses; urandom is used to vary repeated replies.
open_questions: FREE::2A56 is used for registration/management in several places (enter/leave and during use) but its exact semantics (what resource/registry it manipulates) are inferred, not confirmed.

The exact behaviors and parameters of many spawned METHOD::#### helpers (e.g., METHOD::15E7, METHOD::091C, METHOD::0E4F) are not present in this file and are only inferred as animation/movement/timer helpers.
The internal purpose and timing semantics of U8P_GUARD1::1033 (spawned on enterFastArea) are only inferred as a short helper; its implementation is not shown here.
Precise boundaries and string content of some ask-menu entries are inferred from comparisons; localized string variants or exact option ordering are not present in this extract.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD1::look
main_function.purpose: Respond when a player inspects/targets the guard: produce an immediate short bark identifying the guard or indicating the guard is dead.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GUARD1::look) triggered when an actor (typically the Avatar) inspects the guard.
The function queries Npc::isDead to select the proper short response.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::look
function_map.role: Short inspection/identity response for the guard NPC

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Handle actor entering guard's proximity: register via engine helper, spawn proximity helper if guard alive

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Handle actor leaving guard's proximity: deregister/cleanup via engine helper

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::use
function_map.role: Full conversation/talk handler: present dialog menu, branch on responses, update flags, spawn animations/timers, and control avatar stasis

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::1033
function_map.role: Brief spawned helper process created on proximity entry (likely timed/auxiliary behavior); implementation not included here

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Check NPC death state used to branch U8P_GUARD1::look and fast-area behavior

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a short helper process when an actor enters the guard's fast area (proximity helper/timer)

external_calls.purpose: Determine whether to use the 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman' bark branch
Emit the short identification or dead-line to the inspecting process/player
Register/manage per-actor fast-area state or resources when an actor enters proximity (inferred registration call)
Deregister/cleanup per-actor fast-area state on exit (inferred cleanup call)
Present a multiple-choice dialog to the player and receive the selected response for branching
Produce dialog lines / speech bubbles for the guard's responses throughout conversation
Obtain the player's or NPC's name to build personalized responses like 'I am <name>.'
Temporarily disable player controls during some dialog/interaction sequences
Introduce random variation into repeated reply selection
Query guard facing direction to choose direction-dependent animation or movement spawn
Check last animation set to conditionally run an animation sequence
Compute direction to a target position/item to orient spawned animation helpers
Command the guard to pathfind/move to a target tile as part of a dialog-linked action
Spawn a timed helper or schedule a delayed side-effect (inferred from spawn-with-delay usage)
Spawn various animation/movement/timer helpers (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::091C, METHOD::0E4F, METHOD::0BCF, etc.) to enact visible behaviors triggered by dialog

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Used to decide whether to include the 'Open the gate!' dialog option; affects available dialog branches

reads.name: guard1job
reads.likely_effect: Read when player requests gate opening to determine if prerequisite job/condition has been completed; influences guard's response

reads.name: guard1met
reads.likely_effect: Read to select first-time meeting vs subsequent dialog branches

reads.name: guard1pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to select terse/angry reply variations and influence dialog flow

reads.name: xavierSitting
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose an alternate short greeting branch in certain flows

writes.name: guard1met
writes.likely_effect: Set to record that the player has properly introduced themself or met the guard; changes future dialog to non-first-time branch

writes.name: guard1pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard becomes annoyed (e.g., challenged); causes shorter/angrier dialog on later interactions

writes.name: guard1town
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player is told/confirmed the city's name; alters future dialog about the city identity

writes.name: xavierSitting
writes.likely_effect: Sometimes cleared/modified during conversation flows to change future greeting behavior (inferred)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the guard NPC's short inspection reaction (GUARD1::look), proximity enter/exit hooks (GUARD1::enterFastArea / GUARD1::leaveFastArea), and the full conversation/talk handler (GUARD1::use).
